name: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ), no aeration (PMC3958174)
description: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ), no aeration — medium reported in PMC3958174 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC3958174 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.formulation_warning: workflow correction sparse; kept original — Complex/undefined natural-juice medium; the paper gives only a partial nutrient description, so no full defined chemistry is available. Corrections vs current record: (1) oxygen changed facultative->anaerobic (explicit 'no aeration' condition); (2) added the carbon source (~10% reducing sugars) and nitrogen/phosphorus/amino-acid/B-vitamin content that the paper states but were previously left entirely in 'uncovered', leaving only O2 mapped. Not rejected because the paper explicitly uses and characterizes CAJ as a fermentation medium, but it remains an undefined feedstock medium.
provenance.verification_evidence: Paper (PMC3958174): crude/pure CAJ used without supplementation. CAJ contains '10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids' plus 'B-vitamins, particularly thiamine, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid', total nitrogen '0.04% w/v'. The 'no aeration' record corresponds to the anaerobic Erlenmeyer-flask condition (37C, 150 rpm); the paper also ran an aerobic (2 vvm) CAJ f

uncovered.name: reducing sugars (glucose + fructose)
